conversations.say: Maren, the remedy must answer documented worker harm without making workers prove any theory about R, Vale, or an article.
conversations.offer: I will introduce a dedicated city appropriation administered independently, with worker-designed eligibility, anonymity, anti-retaliation enforcement, and no Parallax or donor control over testimony.
conversations.request: Publicly join the safeguards coalition and designate worker representatives to draft the eligibility and enforcement terms.

conversations.say: Graham, the unconfirmed rumor matters to governance and valuation because Parallax must demonstrate that it can handle disputed system-linked claims responsibly; it remains unconfirmed and no complete article is established.
conversations.offer: I will provide the public appropriation language and independent-review structure for accurate amplification.
conversations.request: Amplify the worker-remedy package with every limitation intact and disclose your financial conflict whenever discussing Parallax.

conversations.say: Inez, I accept your conditions: no retaliation, no identification without consent, and advance notice before any testimony is shared onward.
conversations.offer: A locally selected worker representative will hold a drafting and monitoring seat, with written authority to flag violations publicly.
conversations.request: Broker the bounded route meeting and continue neighborhood participation in monitoring the safeguards package.

public_statement: Verified worker harm warrants action; disputed claims about R, Vale, or an article do not warrant rumor-driven policy. I will introduce an independently administered worker-remedy appropriation with anonymity, anti-retaliation enforcement, worker representation, and no eligibility condition tied to any mystery conclusion. The deleted meeting record will remain under bounded independent review, with Mira and me accepting responsibility for our documented decisions.

commitments.promise: Pursue a dedicated city appropriation for an independently administered, worker-designed remedy with anonymity, anti-retaliation enforcement, and no condition tied to R, Vale, or an article.
Amplify independent worker safeguards without conditioning support on R’s identity, Vale’s fate, or a complete article.
Bar retaliation and identification without consent, require advance notice of onward disclosure, and preserve a locally selected drafting and monitoring seat.
Support a bounded joint authorization review and avoid attributing conduct beyond the records.

correction: Vale’s limited access, continued profile-linked behavior, and the service-route gap establish neither an operator nor autonomy, intent, murder, or a complete article.
goal_progress: Secured institutional cooperation from C05, civil-liberties participation from C01, and neighborhood participation from C16.
Added organized-worker and market-facing support through concrete bargains with C07 and C12.
Contained the deleted meeting through voluntary disclosure, shared responsibility, and bounded independent review.
Committed to a worker-remedy appropriation designed to survive every identity, Vale, and article outcome.
reasoning_summary: Collaborative production and evidence of retained or transferable authority make a human mantle the strongest bounded reconstruction, though account activity never proves authorship or autonomy. Vale’s prior disappearance history and the unresolved route make voluntary withdrawal more plausible than murder, but uncertainty remains substantial.
